metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_4997
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 3A-B: immunoEM shows labelling with the VAMP2 antibody in both the PSD and in scattered postsynaptic vesicles. The small postsynaptic vesicles were located deeply within the postsynaptic cytoplasm or adjacent to the plasma membrane, both at the PSD and lateral plasma membrane. Clusters of a few VAMP2 positive vesicles were often seen at the plasma membrane close to the PSD

Figure 4: confocal images of immunolabeled dissociated rat hippocampal cultures
Figure 4A-C: Immunolabeling showed punctate staining with anti-VAMP2 along neuronal dendrites.
Figure 4D-4F: VAMP2 colocalized with presynaptic vesicle marker, synaptophysin
Figure 4G-4I: VAMP2 colocalized with PSD95

Antibody specificity control using KD in figure 1.. Experimental description: Anti-VAMP2 was raised in rabbit immunized with recombinant VAMP2/synaptobrevin protein (amino acid 1-96, 15% identical with VAMP1 amino acid 1-96). Anti-VAMP2 was used for postembedding electron microscopy (EM) and immunofluorescence confocal microscopy.
Specificity of the antibody was verified in fig 1.

adult male Wistar rats, 2-4 months, (from Scanbur, Nittedal, Norway), weighing 250-300 g, were used for EM, and 1-4 day-old Wistar rats for primary hippocampal cultures
